🔭 /ork:audit-full — two tiers, one skill

audit-full's edge is whole-codebase cross-file reasoning in one 1M context. That breaks past ~125K LOC. Instead of punting to another skill, STEP 1's token estimate now routes to a map-reduce Workflow tier that keeps coverage. Drag the slider to see which tier fires.
repo size 60K LOC

① Single-context DEFAULT

load whole codebase → analyze (sec / arch / deps) → STEP 3.5 refute → report

Cheapest, loses zero boundaries — the right call whenever the repo fits. Opus 4.8 · 1M context.

② Map-reduce SCALE TIER (Workflow)

Each shard keeps cross-file reasoning within it; the synthesis pass recovers cross-shard edges (taint/auth/dep-direction spanning modules) — sharding's blind spot, recovered on purpose. Then the same blind-refuter pass. Committed template: audit-full/workflows/audit-full-mapreduce.mjs.

The routing rule (STEP 1)



      
Why not just always fan out?

      

        
Single-contextMap-reduce

        
cross-file reasoning✅ whole repo, one window⚠️ within shard; cross-shard recovered in synthesis

        
cost✅ one pass❌ N shard agents + refuters

        
ceiling~125K LOC (1M tokens)✅ unbounded (shard count scales)

        
refutationSTEP 3.5 inlinesame engine, in the workflow's Refute phase

      

      
→ single-context stays the default because fanning out trades away the cross-file edge. Map-reduce is the fallback for scale one context can't hold — exactly what the Workflow tool is for.


      
Shared engine guarantees (both tiers)

      
blind refuters · citation-verify · quorum (xhigh) · deterministic CVE/build/test EXEMPT
cross-shard finding a refuter can't reproduce → UPHELD · no-auto-flip (refuted CRITICAL flagged, not dropped)
spawn ceiling 24 → overflow shipped "manual review required" (never silent truncation)
